100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 5, 1926, the British General Strike entered its fifth day, as workers across the nation halted operations in solidarity with coal miners demanding better wages and working conditions. Tensions mounted as government forces prepared to maintain essential services, leading to fears of intensified conflict. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated the future of Prohibition, with calls for reform growing louder amid public discontent.
Updated: Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 08:00:43 AM CDT
